acquisition method: the separated peptides were analyzed in Q Exactive HF-X (Thermo Fisher Scientific, San Jose, CA) for DDA (Data Dependent Acquisition) mode detection.
alkylation reagent: alkylated with 55 mM iodoacetamide (45 min in the dark).
alkylation concentration: alkylated with 55 mM iodoacetamide (45 min in the dark).
cleavage agent: proteins (100 µg) of each sample were digested using trypsin (1:20 w/w, Promega, Madison, USA) at 37 ℃ for 4 h.
fragmentation method: The raw MS/MS data were converted into MGF format by thermo scientific tool Proteome Discoverer, and the exported MGF files were searched using Mascot version 2.3.02 (Matrix Science, London, UK) against human Uniprot database (http://www.uniprot.org). The mass tolerance for precursor ions was set as 10 ppm and the mass tolerance for fragment ions was set as 0.02 Da.
instrument: the separated peptides were analyzed in Q Exactive HF-X (Thermo Fisher Scientific, San Jose, CA) for DDA (Data Dependent Acquisition) mode detection.
labeling: To assess the protein cargo inside EVs secreted from STCs, iTRAQ-labeling quantitative proteomic analysis was performed.
reduction reagent: The mixtures were reduced with 10 mM DTT (37℃ for 30 min) and alkylated with 55 mM iodoacetamide.
reduction concentration: The mixtures were reduced with 10 mM DTT (37℃ for 30 min).
